intro: Novo Nordisk cross-checks hundreds of expense records and healthcare interactions every month. Two systems. No connection. Weeks of delay before an anomaly is caught.

center.title: The gap
center.body: Every month, someone in your team opens two systems, exports data, and spends days looking for discrepancies. Sometimes they find them. Sometimes they don't.

solution.title: How it works.
solution.intro: The platform connects both systems, applies every compliance rule automatically, and flags what needs a human decision. In hours, not weeks.

pillars.title: Intelligent data integration
pillars.body: SAP Concur and NovoVision connected into a single automated workflow. Daily scheduled sync. Resilient ingestion via Azure Service Bus.

pillars.title: AI-powered compliance engine
pillars.body: Document Intelligence reads every ticket: vendor, date, amounts, alcohol. Image Analysis v4 counts attendees and detects prohibited items in photos. Automatically correlated with NovoVision activity records.

pillars.title: Business rules
pillars.body: Every Farmaindustria rule codified. Alcohol limits, spend caps, disallowed vendors, required evidence per activity type. Cross-source correlation. Risk scoring with full audit trail.

pillars.title: Operational dashboard
pillars.body: Alert inbox prioritized by severity and SLA. Case explorer with advanced filters. Each case shows the triggering rule, AI confidence, linked evidence, and four decision options: Validate, Not valid, Request evidence, Close.

pillars.title: Enterprise infrastructure
pillars.body: Azure-native. Azure AD SSO. Key Vault for secrets. CI/CD pipelines. Full audit trail for Farmaindustria inspections.

prototype.title: An early prototype already exists.
prototype.body: It helps make the proposal more tangible by showing a first version of the interface, navigation and key platform flows.

scope.title: What we build, phase by phase.
scope.intro: Two tracks running in parallel: design and development. Each phase has a clear deliverable. Nothing moves forward without it.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Compliance Alignment
phases.deliverable: Functional map + acceptance criteria per module
details: Kickoff with Legal (LECQ) and Compliance, supported by an IT interlocutor.
Review of rules, evidences, and KPI/SLA definitions.
Mapping of available data fields relevant to the UI.

phases.title: Phase 2 — UX Definition
phases.deliverable: High-fidelity wireframes + rules scheme visible in UI
details: Information architecture across all modules: Login, Inbox, Cases, Detail, Matrix, Projects, Metrics, Settings.
User flows and system states modelled.

phases.title: Phase 3 — UI Concept & Foundations
phases.deliverable: Design foundations — typography, spacing, component base
details: Visual language, severity color coding, hierarchy of risks.
Accessibility principles and state handling: empty, error, loading.

phases.title: Phase 4 — MVP Design & Prototyping
details: Detailed design of all screens and states.
Navigable prototype built for validation with your team.

phases.title: Phase 5 — Documentation & Handoff
phases.deliverable: Handoff package — Figma specs + QA checklist
details: Design system documentation and usage patterns.
QA acceptance criteria defined per module.

phases.title: Phase 6 — QA Design Support
details: Visual and functional reviews during development.
Microcopy adjustments, accessibility compliance validated.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Data Preparation
details: API routes defined for SAP Concur and NovoVision.
Sample dataset collected — ~100 tickets and associated photos.
Data completeness validated against key monitoring rules.
AI confidence acceptance criteria defined for document parsing and image recognition.
Gaps in data availability identified, mitigations planned.

phases.title: Phase 2 — Data Acquisition
details: Azure Service Bus queues designed for ticket, photo, and event data.
Azure Functions triggers implemented for incoming messages.
Data schema consistency ensured across sources.
Logging and retry policies added for resilience.

phases.title: Phase 3 — AI Pipelines
phases.deliverable: Working pipeline validated with test data
details: Document Intelligence: structured extraction from tickets — vendor, date, total, alcohol items.
Image Analysis v4: people counting, object detection, captioning — beverages, meeting setup.
Outputs normalized into a unified format.
Error handling for AI timeouts and low-confidence results.

phases.title: Phase 4 — Business Rules & Correlation
details: Ticket rules: alcohol limits, daily/monthly spend caps, disallowed vendors — Glovo, supermarkets.
Photo rules: attendee matching, prohibited item detection.
Document rules: required evidence per activity type, event detail matching.
Temporal correlation between tickets, photos, and NVE records.
Risk scores and metadata generated for every alert.

phases.title: Phase 5 — Dashboard & UX
details: Next.js dashboard: alert listings, filters by date, type, risk score, and employee.
Detail view per alert with linked tickets, photos, and records.
Real-time notifications for high-risk cases.
Azure Functions endpoints serving dashboard data securely.

phases.title: Phase 6 — Security, Monitoring & Deployment
details: Azure AD integrated for authentication and role-based permissions.
Secrets managed via Azure Key Vault.
Alert thresholds configured — financial limits, repetition rules.
Infrastructure as code. CI/CD pipelines via Azure DevOps / GitHub Actions.

phases.title: Phase 7 — QA
details: End-to-end functional and integration tests.
User acceptance testing with Novo Nordisk compliance and IT teams.
AI outputs validated against manual checks.
Full technical and functional documentation delivered.

pipelines.title: Two pipelines. Tickets and photos.
pipelines.intro: Each one runs automatically. Each one generates evidence. Each one flags what needs a decision.

team.title: A team built for this project.
intro: We work inside your team's rhythm — same tools, same channels, same weekly cadence. Not as a vendor you check in with once a week.
One shared context from day one. A living roadmap that adapts weekly. Knowledge that never gets lost when the team reconfigures.

comparison.title: You've worked with a consultancy before.
comparison.subtitle: Here's what's different.

cases.title: We've done this before.
cases.intro: Cargill. dormakaba. Inditex. Different industries, same challenge: complex data, high stakes, no margin for error.

items.title: Cargill: The flight radar of maritime logistics
items.label: Data · Anomaly detection · Global scale
body: Cargill needed real-time visibility across a global fleet handling sensitive logistics data.
We built a platform that consolidated contract management, vessel tracking, and automated invoice validation into a single system.
The impact was significant enough that the platform led to a multi-million dollar acquisition by Veson Nautical.

items.title: Cargill: The camera sees what the schedule doesn't
items.label: Computer vision · Predictive analytics
body: Older vessels accumulate biological growth on their hulls. It doesn't trigger alarms — but it quietly increases fuel consumption per voyage.
We built a system that tracks each voyage continuously and flags the gap between what the data predicts and what actually happens.
The diagnosis that used to require weeks of manual review now happens while the ship is still at sea.

items.title: dormakaba: +40 countries. One headless website.
items.label: Contentful · Headless CMS · Enterprise
body: dormakaba needed to overhaul a complex, multilingual corporate site with dozens of local variants.
We replaced their legacy stack with a modern headless architecture — now considered a global benchmark in Contentful implementation.
Three years later, our team of 9 engineers is still their tech partner.
items.quote: We were looking at how easy it would be to work and communicate with them. And Tailor Hub fits that.

roi.title: What changes from day one.
roi.intro: These aren't projections. They're what happens when manual cross-checking gets replaced by a system that runs every night.
metrics: unified monitoring interface for all expense and event data

investment.title: The investment.
investment.intro: Three phases. The same senior team throughout. No handoffs, no knowledge loss between stages.
phases: Deployed functional platform — Backend + AI + Frontend
postLaunch: Senior Software Engineer (32h) + DevOps (16h) — the same team that built the system.
Base infra + AI services. Estimate; varies with usage.

roadmap.title: 9 sprints. 16 weeks.
roadmap.intro: Design and development run in parallel from sprint 3 onwards. 15-day cycles throughout.

assumptions.title: Project assumptions.
items: The solution deploys within Novo Nordisk's existing Azure infrastructure.
API credentials for SAP Concur and NovoVision (NVE) provided by Novo Nordisk.
Minimum 100 real tickets and 100 representative photos for initial testing.
One IT interlocutor and one compliance/legal contact designated from day one.
Daily cron job retrieving previous day's data.
Azure Face API excluded from scope; the system counts people but does not identify them.
New features and integrations handled under a separate agreement.

contact.title: Next step.
contact.body: The next step is a 45-minute working session. No slides — just your data and your rules on the table.
contact.name: Diego Mendez
